I would like you to implement a module named TopModule with the following
interface. All input and output ports are one bit unless otherwise
specified.

 - input  A (8 bits)
 - output B (16 bits)

Design a computational unit circuit that multiplies A[7:0] by the constant
0xFB (binary 11111011). You should implement this using as few resources
as possible.


Based on the problem description above, the code below in TopModule has bug, please fix that:
```
module TopModule(
    input  [7:0]    A,
    output [15:0]   B
    );

//*************code***********//

    assign B = (A<<8)-(A<<2)+A;
//*************code***********//

endmodule
```

======= VCD OUTPUT BEGIN =======
$date
	Mon Dec  1 21:25:34 2025
$end
$version
	Icarus Verilog
$end
$timescale
	1ps
$end
$scope module tb $end
$scope module stim1 $end
$var wire 1 ! clk $end
$upscope $end
$upscope $end
$scope module tb $end
$var wire 1 " tb_mismatch $end
$upscope $end
$scope module tb $end
$var reg 1 ! clk $end
$upscope $end
$scope module tb $end
$var wire 8 # A [7:0] $end
$upscope $end
$scope module tb $end
$var wire 16 $ B_ref [15:0] $end
$upscope $end
$scope module tb $end
$var wire 16 % B_dut [15:0] $end
$upscope $end
$enddefinitions $end
$comment Show the parameter values. $end
$dumpall
$end
#0
$dumpvars
b0 %
b0 $
b0 #
0"
0!
$end
